response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a broad full-stack web development background with tangential geospatial exposure through the GISCollective project, but critically lacks the core technical requirements for this role. C++ experience is limited to an entry-level position over 25 years ago and a bachelor thesis project, with no subsequent commercial C++ work. The career is dominated by TypeScript and Node.js. While there is some distributed-systems exposure at Sociomantic, there is no evidence of experience with routing tile generation, navigation pipeline architecture, or production geospatial data infrastructure of the kind described in the job description.
strengths: GIS and geospatial domain exposure through the GISCollective open-source platform for map data collection
Distributed systems experience handling high-throughput, low-latency workloads (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic)
Long track record of technical adaptability, TDD discipline, and working across the full stack
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust experience — the primary language throughout the entire career is TypeScript/Node.js
C++ exposure is from 2000–2001 (National Instruments) and a bachelor thesis project, with no commercial C++ work since
No routing, navigation guidance, or tile-generation experience — GIS work is data collection for NGOs, not production routing pipelines

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation pipeline experience
